DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER-CA, LLC, RASIER, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS HIBAH AHMED AND WASIB MUHAMMED TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS: THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE CASE IS NOT AT ISSUE AS DEFENDANT RODRIGUES HAS NOT YET BEEN SERVED, DESPITE THE DILIGENT EFFORTS OF PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL. WITH THE TRIAL DATE NOW HAVING BEEN CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 2022, THERE IS NO PREJUDICE TO MOVING PARTIES BY DELAYING THE DEPOSITIONS UNTIL SUCH TIME AS WHEN SERVICE ON ALL DEFENDANTS IS COMPLETED OR ABANDONED. PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH TO ARRIVE AT AN AGREEABLE SCHEDULE AS TO WHEN SERVICE WILL BE ACCOMPLISHED AND SCHEDULING OF THE PLAINTIFFS' DEPOSITIONS. BOTH PARTIES' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. PLAINTIFFS/PREVAILING PARTY TO SEND PROPOSED ORDER IN BOTH WORD AND PDF FORMAT, TRACKING THE TENTATIVE RULING VIA EMAIL TO NOAH@NDLLEGAL.COM.

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc.; Rasier, Llc; And Rasier-Ca, Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Hibah Ahmed And Wasib Muhammed To Appear For Deposition; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-25-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Pavel Krepkiy (pavel@dubrovskylawyers.com) of Anna Dubrovsky Law Group, Inc. for Plaintiffs.
Dustin Cameron (510-444-6800) of Burnham Brown for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER-CA, LLC, RASIER, LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Hibah Ahmed And Wasib Muhammed To Appear For Deposition; Request For Monetary Sanctions:

The motion is DENIED. The case is not at issue as Defendant Rodrigues has not yet been served, despite the diligent efforts of plaintiffs' counsel. With the trial date now having been continued to February 2022, there is no prejudice to moving parties by delaying the depositions until such time as when service on all defendants is completed or abandoned. Parties are to meet and confer in good faith to arrive at an agreeable schedule as to when service will be accomplished and scheduling of the plaintiffs' depositions. Both parties' request for sanctions are DENIED.

Plaintiffs/Prevailing party to send proposed order in both Word and PDF format, tracking the tentative ruling via email to noah@ndllegal.com.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-16-2020 Set For Trial On Mar-22-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Off Calendar Feb-28-2020 Continued To Apr-29-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
